bullets: Christians in China facing ongoing arrests, indictments, and detention for house worship, religious instruction, resisting surveillance, and developing devotional resources.
Dozens of Jehovah's Witnesses in Eritrea, including minors, detained without charge for months or years for peacefully practicing their faith and following their conscience.
A 13-year-old Christian girl in Pakistan held through a forged forced marriage while her family pursues justice against a legal ruling dismissing their petition.
Naga and Kuki Christian communities in India grieving the mutilation and killing of six abducted tribal believers, including pastors, amid escalating ethnic violence.
Civilians across southern and eastern Lebanon — thousands ordered to evacuate amid intensifying airstrikes, at least 3,711 killed and 11,483 wounded since March, with no firm ceasefire in effect.
Palestinians in Gaza — nearly 1,000 killed and more than 3,000 wounded since a ceasefire was declared, with daily strikes continuing on refugee camps, neighborhoods, and civilian gathering points.
Nearly 50,000 displaced people in Myanmar facing critical shortages of food and shelter, alongside a foreign national detained by military authorities.
Communities across Nigeria — kidnapping survivors, hostages still in captivity, families targeted by fraud schemes, and villages struck by armed groups in multiple regions.
Civilians in Sudan's Kordofan province — 14 people killed in an attack by armed forces amid an ongoing civil war that has displaced millions.
Residents of South Sudan's Eastern Equatoria facing deadly ambushes on roads used daily for trade, transport, and access to medical care.
Kenyan security forces and border communities — soldiers and officers wounded in repeated al-Shabaab attacks on military positions near the Somalia border.
Somali communities near security camps targeted in al-Shabaab assaults, with ongoing militant pressure on the country's security infrastructure.
Haitian civilians and professionals living under pervasive gang control — including the high-profile abduction of a senior security official in the capital.
Residents of a Johannesburg settlement where at least 12 people were killed and nine wounded in a coordinated late-night attack.
Immigrant families in Northern Ireland burned out of their homes and living in fear following nights of anti-immigrant rioting, and protesters calling for peace.
Civilians across Ukraine — residents of eastern cities struck by airstrikes, ongoing infrastructure attacks, and communities on both sides enduring years of war.
People across Russia and Ukraine caught between escalating strikes on energy infrastructure and continued missile and drone attacks on populated areas.
Congolese protesters and opposition figures in the capital wounded in clashes over proposed constitutional changes.
Travelers and civilians in South Sudan's Eastern Equatoria at risk from ambushes on roads vital for daily movement and commerce.
Journalists detained in Mali amid a pattern of press restrictions under military rule, with freedom of expression under growing pressure.
Protesters in Albania clashing with police over coastal development threatening protected wetlands, with tensions continuing in the capital.
Indonesian students protesting fuel price increases and government spending concerns amid rising economic anxiety.
Kenyan families mourning three people killed — including a teenager — during clashes over a planned quarantine facility.
Ultra-Orthodox communities and police across Israel — dozens injured in recurring unrest over military conscription enforcement.
Venezuelan and Latin American communities affected by Tren de Aragua gang violence spanning multiple countries.
Civilians in the Gulf region and broader Middle East caught in unresolved US-Iran tensions and fragile, unsigned ceasefire negotiations.
People across Iraq's western regions — communities still living under the threat of ISIS networks despite ongoing security operations.
Families in Mexico mourning elected officials killed by armed groups, and rural community leaders across the country living under threat of cartel violence.
Immigrant families in the United States — including children — separated from parents through immigration enforcement actions.
Activists in the United Kingdom facing terrorism-related charges and imprisonment for protest activity, with civil liberties concerns growing across the country.

More than 700 confirmed Ebola cases and 149 deaths in the Democratic Republic of Congo, with contact tracing gaps leaving the true extent of the outbreak unknown and health workers under severe pressure
Around 32,000 people displaced and at least 37 killed by earthquakes in the Philippines, with isolated villages facing hunger and urgent need for food airlifts
Families across Pakistan mourning more than 835 deaths as widespread monsoon flooding destroys homes and cuts off communities from emergency aid
At least 15 people killed when a mudslide struck a rural guesthouse in China during heavy rainfall
Roughly 62,000 people displaced by drought in Somalia, with families abandoning homes in search of water and food as the humanitarian crisis deepens
Cyclone-hit communities in Madagascar mourning at least 20 dead and 33 injured, with ongoing recovery needs as international aid begins arriving
Families along the Euphrates River in Syria, already fragile from years of conflict, displaced by rising floodwaters affecting children and families
Residents across Pakistan, Canada, the United States, and Costa Rica navigating ongoing flood emergencies ranging from flash flooding and river surges to storm-driven evacuations
Families in northeastern India mourning deaths caused by landslides and a vehicle plunge into a gorge amid dangerous monsoon-season terrain
Residents near active volcanoes in Indonesia and Papua New Guinea facing ongoing ash eruptions and the threat of intensifying submarine volcanic activity
Communities across fifteen Nigerian states, Poland, Finland, Hong Kong, and the central United States in the path of severe storms bringing dangerous winds, hail, and flooding risk
Residents across the western United States enduring dangerous heatwaves with temperatures forecast to reach 110 degrees Fahrenheit and limited overnight relief
Communities in the Philippines, Bhutan, Chile, Mexico, and Indonesia following a series of earthquakes ranging from magnitude 5 to 6 across multiple seismically active regions
Sixty-two thousand displaced Somalis and drought-stricken communities facing rising hunger and dwindling water access as dry conditions persist
Residents of Newfoundland facing a meningococcal disease outbreak, and communities in Kenya mourning three deaths including a teenager caught in unrest over an Ebola quarantine facility
Around 900 tourists evacuated and communities in southern Italy and Kazakhstan facing wildfire danger amid dry and volatile conditions
Residents of the United Kingdom facing potential water shortages as prolonged drought pushes fourteen water companies toward hosepipe restrictions
